channel.title: Nfl Draft on Benjamin Robinson - Personal Website & Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Nfl Draft on Benjamin Robinson - Personal Website & Blog

item.title: Riser or Faller? Mock Draft Stock and the NFL Combine
item.description: Run, Rich, Run! alt text For people who follow the NFL Draft, the draft process can be readily demarcated into managable chunks: the end of the college football season bleeds into draft declarations which then transition smoothly to the playoffs and the Super Bowl to the college All Star games and finally to where we are now: the NFL Scouting Combine. The Combine marks the last major milestone (pretty much) before we reach the draft in all its glory in late April.

item.title: Have Rumors of Tua's Demise Been Greatly Exaggerated
item.description: Coming into the 2019 college football season, Tua Tagovailoa was riding high. He was a 2018 Heisman Trophy finalist, had just led his Alabama Crimson Tide to the championship game of the 2019 College Football Playoff, and was widely believed to be the front runner to be selected first overall in the 2020 NFL Draft. Then on a late fall afternoon in Starkville, it all came tumbling down. With Alabama holding a comfortable 35-7 lead over Mississippi State with about 3 minutes left in the first half, Tua sustained a hip dislocation that ended his season.

item.title: Grinding the Mocks: My 2019 Meta Mock Draft
item.description: Draft Day Has Arrived! Oh the joys of the movie Draft Day featuring Kevin Costner Tonight is the night! Yes, ladies and gentleman, the 2019 NFL Draft is upon us. With no further, ado, since you can reference my other blog posts about my theory of action and my methodology and performance in the 2018 NFL Draft. Here you go! According to my research, there are two metrics to look closely at out of the ones I’ve studied: the weighted average where we weight each mock draft by how close it is made to the day of the NFL Draft and the output of prediction made using a Loess regression model, a method that smooths out data instead of trying to fit a line of best fit through our data to better indentify trends.

item.title: Grinding the Mocks: The Signal and the Noise
item.description: The Approach alt text At the time this post will go live, the draft will be 10 days away! With that in mind, its time for the second of my series of blog posts about using mock draft data to predict the NFL Draft. In my last blog post, I layed out my theory of mock drafts and how I think about using mock drafts as a way of assessing a player’s stock throughout the NFL Draft process.

item.title: Grinding the Mocks: A Theory of Mock Drafts
item.description: The Nostalgia Factor As an analytical-minded person who was simultaneously consuming my third straight day of television coverage of the National Football League draft, the following thought crept into my brain: “why do I care so much about the NFL Draft?” After searching long and hard for an answer, I have come to believe that one reason I’m so interested in the draft is because at its heart, the draft is a source of optimism in a world where the team you root for might not always win on the field.
